title: Harden test suite — coverage gaps, quality issues, and missing scenarios
description:
## Problem

The test suite has coverage gaps, unused fixtures, and quality issues. Audited 2026-02-22 against current code — 7 items from the original bead are done (from prior refactors), 6 are moot. 32 items remain.

## A. Coverage Gaps — Untested Code Paths (22 items)

### `src/dagshund/__init__.py` — `parse_plan`
- Empty string input (`""`) — exercises `json.JSONDecodeError` path
- Numeric JSON literal (`"42"`) — should raise "must be an object"
- Boolean/null JSON literal (`"true"`, `"null"`) — should raise "must be an object"

### `src/dagshund/cli.py` — `_read_plan`
- Directory instead of file — `IsADirectoryError` (subclass of `OSError`)

### `src/dagshund/cli.py` — `main`
- Invalid JSON piped via stdin → DagshundError on stderr + exit code 1
- `--output` to unwritable directory
- Stdin + `--output` flag combined (stdin → HTML export is completely untested)

### `src/dagshund/text.py` — `_render_resource`
- UPDATE_ACTIONS besides `"update"`: test `recreate`, `resize`, `update_id` as full resource entries through `_render_resource` end-to-end
- Field change with no `old`/`new` on a non-skip action (the `(False, False)` match arm)
- Field change with missing `action` key — exercises `change.get("action", "")` fallback

### `src/dagshund/text.py` — other functions
- `_count_by_action({})` — empty input
- `_print_resource_groups` with multiple resource types (only tested with single type "jobs")
- `_print_resource_groups` with empty dict
- `_print_summary` with empty plan
- `_print_summary` with all-same-action
- `_format_value` with exactly 80-char string (boundary, should NOT truncate) and 81-char string (should truncate)
- `_parse_resource_key("")` — empty string

### `src/dagshund/text.py` — `render_text` with fixtures
- Test with `invalid-plan.json` (unknown action type + malformed changes)
- Test with `mixed-plan.json` (create/update/delete/skip mix)
- Test with `sample-plan.json` (single create-only plan)

### `src/dagshund/browser.py`
- `_escape_for_script_tag("")` — empty string
- `_inject_plan` where plan data contains the PLACEHOLDER string as a value
- `render_browser` overwriting an existing file

### Cross-module / Integration
- Subprocess test with `--output` flag (HTML output mode end-to-end)
- Subprocess test piping stdin instead of file argument
- `render_text` + color env var interaction (FORCE_COLOR/NO_COLOR)

## B. Unused Fixtures — Wire Them Up (3 items)
- `fixtures/mixed-plan.json` — create, update, delete, skip actions (use in `render_text` tests)
- `fixtures/sample-plan.json` — minimal create-only plan (use in `render_text` tests)
- `fixtures/invalid-plan.json` — invalid action type + malformed changes (use in `render_text` tests)

## C. Test Quality Fixes (7 items)

### Replace mock with monkeypatch
- `test_cli.py` ~line 141: `test_main_browser_flag_opens_browser` uses `unittest.mock.patch("webbrowser.open")` → use `monkeypatch.setattr(webbrowser, "open", ...)`

### Replace manual try/finally with monkeypatch
- `test_browser.py` ~line 24: `test_find_template_raises_when_missing` manually saves/restores `browser_mod.__file__` → use `monkeypatch.setattr`

### Parametrize opportunities
- `test_browser.py`: four `_escape_for_script_tag` tests → single parametrized test
- `test_init.py`: `test_parse_plan_array_raises` + `test_parse_plan_string_raises` → single parametrized test

### Fix brittle assertion
- `test_browser.py` ~line 91: `test_inject_plan_uses_compact_json` checks `'", ' not in result` on entire HTML string — extract just the injected JSON portion

### Naming convention
- `test_cli_version_flag` → `test_main_version_flag_prints_version`
- `test_cli_text_mode_with_file` → `test_main_text_mode_with_file_prints_output`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 22 coverage gaps have tests
- All three unused fixtures used in at least one test
- All 7 quality issues fixed
- `just check` passes
- No `from unittest.mock import` in any test file
close_reason: All 32 items addressed: 22 coverage gaps, 3 unused fixtures wired up, 7 quality fixes. text.py at 100% coverage. No unittest.mock imports remain.

title: Structural cleanup: barrels, shared glow helper, error boundary, minor hardening
description:
Ten low-severity structural findings from TS review:

1. **Dead barrel exports**: 5 of 6 index.ts barrel files (utils, types, hooks, components, parser) are never imported from. Either adopt them consistently or remove them.

2. **Incomplete barrel**: graph/index.ts (the one barrel that IS used) doesn't re-export extractResourceType, isJobEntry, etc. that components import directly.

3. **Duplicated glow styles**: Four node components (job-node, task-node, resource-node, resource-group-node) have identical inline style logic for boxShadow (hover/selection) and opacity (dimming). Extract a shared helper.

4. **Two-pass array partition** (build-resource-graph.ts:336-343): UC vs workspace entries separated with two .filter() calls. One pass would be cleaner.

5. **hasFittedRef fragility** (flow-canvas.tsx:71): Ref never reset — safe because component remounts on plan change, but add a comment noting the assumption.

6. **Empty tasks fallback** (extract-tasks.ts:59-66): resolveTaskEntries falls back to remote_state for explicit empty tasks array. Add a comment noting this is intentional for the Databricks domain.

7. **ELK singleton** (layout-graph.ts:13-21): No crash recovery. Add a comment noting this is acceptable for a visualization tool.

8. **No React error boundary** (App.tsx): If React Flow throws during render, entire app crashes. Add an error boundary around the canvas.

9. **FlowCanvas size** (flow-canvas.tsx, ~280 lines): Permitted as orchestration but could extract edge-styling useMemo or filtering logic into custom hooks.

10. **Two-pass partition for UC/workspace resources** can be combined with item 4 above.

title: refactor: make GraphNode a discriminated union by nodeKind
description: R3-MED-8. GraphNode in types/graph-types.ts is a single type for 4 node kinds (job, task, resource, resource-group). Fields like taskKey, taskChangeSummary, external are undefined for most kinds. Components do runtime checks like data.nodeKind === 'job' && data.taskChangeSummary \!== undefined. CLAUDE.md says 'discriminated unions over type assertions.' Fix: split into JobNodeData, TaskNodeData, ResourceNodeData, ResourceGroupNodeData with a GraphNode = union. Each variant only has fields relevant to that kind. This makes impossible states unrepresentable. Flagged by: Architect (LOW), Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: Extract node+edge building from buildChainGraph inner loop
description: The inner loop of buildChainGraph is 54 lines handling both resource node construction and parent edge resolution. Extract into a helper function for readability. The function works correctly as-is — this is a readability improvement.

title: Handle xdg-open/browser-open failure gracefully
description: Bun.spawn([detectOpenCommand(), ...]) in src/index.ts:70 and src/cli.ts:221 has no error handling. On Ubuntu without a default browser, xdg-open dumps a wall of 'not found' errors. Should catch the failure and print a friendly message like 'Could not open browser. Visit http://... manually'.

title: Python dev tooling: ruff and pyrefly
description: Add Python linting (ruff) and type checking (pyrefly) to the project. Configure in pyproject.toml, add just targets, integrate into just check pipeline.

title: Strengthen is_resource_changes TypeGuard or clarify naming
description: is_resource_changes in types.py:15-17 only checks isinstance(value, dict) but the name implies structural validation of ResourceChanges (dict[str, dict[str, Any]]). Either rename to something like assume_resource_changes to clarify it's a type-system-only narrowing, or add at least one level of structural checking (keys are strings, values are dicts).

title: perf: improve hover rendering performance in graph views
description: Multiple compounding performance issues on hover: (1) No React.memo on any of the 4 node components — every hover re-renders all nodes via context. (2) useNodeConnections receives new object literals every render in all 4 node components — hoist to module-level constants. (3) styledEdges spreads every edge into a new object on hover and unnecessarily spreads in the null/null case — return baseEdges directly when no styling needed. (4) Event handlers recreated every render — wrap in useCallback. Files: job-node.tsx, task-node.tsx, resource-node.tsx, resource-group-node.tsx, App.tsx, resources-view.tsx.

title: Audit Lakebase/Postgres hierarchy nomenclature and structure
description:
Current naming may be wrong from Databricks' perspective:
- 'Provisioned' → instances (database_instances)
- 'Autoscale' → Postgres (postgres)
- Need to verify the full hierarchy for both flavors is correct
- Instance chaining exists as a parent-child relationship we may be missing
- Cross-reference with Databricks docs to get the naming and nesting right
- This affects node labels, group node structure, and how we build the subgraph in build-resource-graph.ts
close_reason: Audited against Databricks docs. Root labels renamed, badges updated to match bundle resource type names. Filed dagshund-8zkh for instance chaining, dagshund-vruo for badge design, dagshund-fiyh for layout weights.

title: fix: formatValue conflates undefined and null
description: formatValue in utils/format-value.ts returns 'null' for both undefined and null. In diff context, field absent (undefined) vs field explicitly null are different. Fix: return 'undefined' or '<absent>' for undefined, 'null' for null.

title: Inline spread copies create unstable array refs on every hover
description: flow-canvas.tsx:389-390 — [...baseNodes] and [...styledEdges] create new mutable array copies on every render. FlowCanvas re-renders on hover (~every 50ms during mouse movement). The styledEdges spread is partially redundant since useStyledEdges already returns a new array when styles change. Fix: replace inline spreads with useMemo(() => [...arr], [arr]) to only copy when the source reference changes.

title: Add global / shortcut to focus search bar
close_reason: Added global / shortcut with offsetParent visibility guard

title: style: default value format to YAML instead of JSON
Improve detail panel formatting for create/delete diffs
description: When viewing a created (added) resource in the detail panel, the per-field cards show each field's value via CreateOnlyView which just calls formatValue() on the raw value. For object/array values this dumps everything as a single + blob with no structure. Example: check_duplicates shows 'check_duplicatesadded' in the header (name and badge run together), and nested fields like depends_on, notebook_task, task_key are all inlined as flat text rather than displayed as labeled key-value entries. Need to: (1) fix spacing between the field label and the diff state badge in ObjectStateCard/StateFieldRow headers, (2) render object values in CreateOnlyView/DeleteOnlyView with per-key breakdown instead of a single blob.

title: capture action severity ordering for future use
description: The CLI defines a severity ordering for actions: Undefined(0) < Skip(1) < Resize(2) < Update(3) < UpdateWithID(4) < Create(5) < Recreate(6) < Delete(7). This ordering is not captured in dagshund. Could be useful for sorting resources by impact, showing most-severe-action badges on groups, or priority-based rendering. No current use case but worth having the data available.

title: Extract lateral handle ID strings into named constants
description: Handle ID strings (lateral-top, lateral-top-out, lateral-bottom, lateral-bottom-out) are raw literals repeated across layout-graph.ts, resource-node.tsx, hierarchy-node.tsx, and use-node-dimming.ts. Extract into named constants to prevent typo-induced silent failures.

title: feat: static HTML export mode for CI/CD and local viewing
description:
Add a CLI mode that renders a plan into a self-contained HTML file with all JS/CSS inlined. The plan JSON is embedded as a <script> tag so no server is needed — React boots up and provides the full interactive experience (pan, zoom, click nodes, hover, filter, JSON/YAML toggle).

## Distribution
Ship as an npm/bun package with a bin field in package.json. Users run:
- bunx dagshund plan.json (opens in browser)
- bunx dagshund plan.json -o report.html (saves file for CI artifacts)
- databricks bundle plan -o json | bunx dagshund -o report.html (pipeline-friendly)

No Docker, no server, no runtime infrastructure. CI pipelines already have Node/Bun.

## Implementation
1. New src/cli.ts entry point — arg parsing, reads plan JSON from file arg or stdin
2. Add bin field to package.json pointing to cli.ts
3. CLI runs Bun bundler to produce JS/CSS, inlines everything into a single HTML file
4. Plan JSON injected as window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ script tag
5. Frontend checks window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ first, falls back to fetch(/api/plan) for dev server mode
6. npm publish makes it available via bunx/npx

## Future
Standalone binary via bun build --compile can be added later for environments without Node/Bun.

title: epic: Staff Review R2 — Accessibility
description: 5 accessibility issues from staff review R2. Keyboard nav, ARIA labels, focus rings, aria-pressed, color-blind support.

title: fix: remove redundant identity label from array diff display
collectPhantomDatabaseInstances scans all entry types
description: collectPhantomDatabaseInstances scans all entries for database_instance_name, but DATABASE_INSTANCE_SPEC only processes synced_database_tables and database_catalogs. If a future resource type also has that field, phantom nodes would be created with no matching lateral edge. Fix: filter by the same source types as DATABASE_INSTANCE_SPEC.

title: Add demo screenshots for phantom nodes and lateral edges
description: Add screenshots or visual demos showing phantom nodes and lateral edges to the GitLab Pages demo site or project docs. These features are hard to explain in text — images would help users understand what they do.

title: Add LICENSE file (MIT)
description: LICENSE file created, pyproject.toml updated. Ready to commit.

title: Extract separable components from build-resource-graph.ts
description:
build-resource-graph.ts was the largest JS file in the codebase (~800 lines). Extracted conceptually independent sections where boundaries were clean.

## Done
- **Phantom collectors** → `collect-phantom-nodes.ts` (138 lines): `collectPhantomDatabaseInstances`, `collectPhantomAppDependencies`, `resolveAppPhantomRef`. Shared edge helpers (`buildEdge`, `filterDefinedEdges`) moved to `graph-types.ts`. File went from ~800 → 659 lines.

## Evaluated and stopped
The remaining ~659 lines are highly interconnected. The node builders (`buildResourceNode`, `buildRootNode`, etc.) and `resolveEntryEdgeDiffState` are shared by the chain engine, workspace graph builder, depends-on collector, and orchestrator. Extracting any piece requires either a grab-bag shared module, reverse dependencies, or circular imports — all worse than the current file with clear section markers. Same conclusion reached for `layout-graph.ts` (481 lines): two layout pipelines share `getElk`, `toNodeData`, `toFlowEdges`, and sizing constants heavily enough that splitting creates fragmentation without navigability gain.

title: Detail panel truncates long values mid-word
description: Long values in the modified body diff view wrap mid-word even when there's room to fit the full line. Example: `source_table_full_name: dagshund.integrations.partner_rollup` renders as two lines with the break at `partner_\nrollup`. Likely a CSS overflow/word-break issue in the change line or value display components.

title: Break circular dependency between build-resource-graph and extract-lateral-edges
description: extract-lateral-edges.ts imports 4 helper functions (extractResourceState, extractSourceTableFullName, extractStateField, parseThreePartName) from build-resource-graph.ts, while build-resource-graph.ts imports extractLateralEdges from extract-lateral-edges.ts. This creates a file-level circular dependency in graph/. Fix: extract the 4 helpers into graph/extract-resource-state.ts so both files import from the shared module.

title: unknown action type coercion should log a warning
description: actionTypeSchema uses .catch('') which silently coerces unknown action types to empty string (unchanged). If the CLI introduces a new action type, dagshund would silently treat it as unchanged — potentially hiding destructive actions. Consider logging a console.warn when the catch fires, or adding a visual indicator for unrecognized actions.

title: Rename HoverContext to InteractionContext
description: HoverContext/HoverState now carries lateral isolation state (lateralIsolatedIds, lateralNodeIds, isolatedLateralNodeId) plus selection and filter state. The name is a misnomer — rename to InteractionContext/InteractionState or similar.

title: Extract lateral edge state from FlowCanvas
description: flow-canvas.tsx has 5 useMemo blocks for lateral edges (lateralNodeIds, isolatedLateralEdges, activeLateralEdges, visibleEdges, lateralIsolatedIds) plus lateralHandlesByNode. Extract to a useLateralEdgeState hook to reduce component complexity.

title: Split flow-canvas.tsx into proper tier separation
description:
flow-canvas.tsx is 528 lines spanning all three component tiers:

**Tier 3 (Provider):** 10 useState calls, 4 useEffect calls, provides InteractionContext and LateralIsolationContext, subscribes to 6+ custom hooks.

**Tier 2 (Domain):** buildConnectedNodeIds graph traversal, resolvePhantomContext inference, diffStateCounts computation, filterMatchedIds intersection.

**Tier 1 (Dumb UI):** Inline SVG fit-view button, resize handle div with Tailwind, loading overlay, error/empty messages.

Proposed split:
1. FlowCanvasProvider (Tier 3) — owns state, subscribes to hooks, provides contexts, renders FlowCanvasLayout
2. FlowCanvasLayout (Tier 2) — receives nodes/edges/callbacks as props, renders ReactFlow with Panel children
3. Extract FitViewButton (Tier 1) — generic button with expand SVG
4. Extract ResizeHandle (Tier 1) — generic drag handle (or keep inline if trivial)
5. Move buildConnectedNodeIds and resolvePhantomContext to utils (pure functions)

Discovered during React Crusade audit. All 5 squads touched this file — it is the gravitational center of the frontend.

title: Resolve diff prefix map and state resolution pattern duplication
description:
Two redundancies that need a design call before fixing.

## 7. DIFF_STATE_PREFIX vs DIFF_BADGES — parallel diff prefix maps

**Files:** diff-state-styles.ts (DIFF_BADGES / getDiffBadge), detail-panel/task-changes-summary.tsx (DIFF_STATE_PREFIX)

**Problem:** Two maps that do the same thing — map a diff state to a prefix character for display. But they disagree on the "removed" character:
- DIFF_BADGES in diff-state-styles.ts uses Unicode minus sign U+2212 (−)
- DIFF_STATE_PREFIX in task-changes-summary.tsx uses ASCII hyphen-minus U+002D (-)

DIFF_BADGES also includes unchanged: "=" while DIFF_STATE_PREFIX omits it (falls back to " ").

**Open question:** Is the ASCII hyphen vs Unicode minus difference intentional? The task changes summary renders in the detail panel (monospace context where ASCII hyphen aligns fine). The node badges render in the graph view. If the difference is intentional, it needs a comment explaining why. If not, task-changes-summary.tsx should just import and use getDiffBadge.

## 8. "Try new_state first, fall back to remote_state" — 4+ implementations

**Files:** extract-tasks.ts (resolveTaskEntries, resolveJobState), build-resource-graph.ts (extractStateField, extractResourceState)

**Problem:** Four functions independently implement the same strategy:
1. Parse new_state, check if it has the data you want
2. If not, parse remote_state as fallback
3. Return the result or undefined

Each does this with different Zod schemas and different projections:
- resolveTaskEntries: looks for .tasks array
- resolveJobState: looks for state record (minus tasks key)
- extractStateField: looks for a single named field as string
- extractResourceState: returns the whole state record

**Complication:** extract-tasks.ts operates on raw unknown values (newState, remoteState as separate params), while build-resource-graph.ts operates on PlanEntry objects. Aligning the signatures means choosing a convention — either everything takes PlanEntry, or everything takes (newState, remoteState).

**Discussion:** A shared resolveState(newState: unknown, remoteState: unknown): Record<string, unknown> | undefined could serve as the base. Then:
- resolveTaskEntries calls resolveState, extracts .tasks
- resolveJobState calls resolveState, strips .tasks
- extractResourceState wraps resolveState with PlanEntry unpacking
- extractStateField calls extractResourceState, picks one field

This reduces 4 independent parse-and-fallback flows to 1 base + 4 thin projections. But it only saves ~15 lines total and adds a new abstraction layer. Worth doing for consistency, or is the current approach "small functions that each do one thing" already good enough?

title: add typed Zod schemas for PermissionsState and GrantsState
description: Sub-resource state shapes are treated as generic records. The CLI defines specific types: PermissionsState { object_id, permissions[] } and GrantsState { securable_type, full_name, grants[] }. Adding typed schemas would enable better rendering of sub-resource state in the detail panel (e.g., formatting permission entries as a table). Low priority since generic handling works.

title: Document or mitigate debug trace leaking sensitive plan data
description: debug.py:34-60 — when --debug or DAGSHUND_DEBUG is set, the profile hook logs up to 80 chars of function arguments to stderr via _summarize_value. If plan JSON contains sensitive values (API keys, connection strings in Databricks resource state), these could leak to stderr and into CI build logs. Consider documenting this in --help output, or masking values that look like secrets.

title: Add raw JSON toggle button to detail panel
description: Add a toggle button to the detail panel that reveals the raw JSON of the changes/resourceState for the selected node. Useful for debugging and inspecting the exact plan output. Button should toggle between the formatted per-field view and a raw JSON view showing the underlying data.

title: Toggle to hide source-table phantom nodes
description: Add a toolbar toggle that hides source-table:: phantom leaf nodes and their edges from the UC tree. These nodes show spec.source_table_full_name references from synced_database_tables — useful for understanding data lineage but noisy when reviewing deployment diffs. Toggle should filter nodes out entirely (no empty space), not just dim them. Related to dagshund-czfs (broader lateral edge toggle) but distinct: this removes phantom nodes, not just edges between real nodes.

title: Split InteractionContext into hover and selection contexts
description: InteractionContext re-renders every node component on every hover change because hoveredNodeId is part of the context value. Split into a HoverContext (hoveredNodeId, connectedIds) and SelectionContext (selectedNodeId, selectedConnectedIds, filterMatchedIds, etc.) so hover changes only re-render hover-sensitive consumers. This would reduce the O(N) re-render cascade on hover and further improve click reliability on large graphs.
close_reason: won't-fix: all node components consume all InteractionContext fields via useNodeDimming, so splitting into hover/selection contexts wouldn't reduce re-renders. startTransition already handles the priority issue.

title: Add empty-string action handling to Python _ACTIONS dict
description: TypeScript actionTypeSchema includes empty string mapped to unchanged/skip. Python _ACTIONS dict has no entry for empty string — falls through to _DEFAULT_ACTION (unknown, ? prefix). A plan with action: empty string renders as unchanged in browser but unknown in terminal. Fix: add empty string entry to _ACTIONS.

title: Sub-resource keys render as independent nodes
description:
Databricks Asset Bundle plans use 4-segment keys for sub-resources: `resources.TYPE.NAME.SUBRESOURCE` (e.g. `resources.jobs.test_job.permissions`, `resources.jobs.test_job.run_as`). The codebase assumes exactly 3 segments (`resources.TYPE.NAME`), causing sub-resources to render as independent top-level nodes.

**Revised approach (v2):** Don't filter sub-resources out — fold them into their parent resource entry. Sub-resource data (permissions, run_as config, changes) must remain visible in the parent's detail panel.

**Merge strategy:**
- Sub-resource changes get prefixed into parent's changes as `subresource_name.field_name` (e.g. `permissions.permission_level`)
- Sub-resource remote_state folds into parent's resource state display
- Sub-resource depends_on pointing to parent is structural (drop)
- Sub-resource depends_on pointing to external resources becomes phantom nodes / lateral edges on the parent

**Reproducer:** `dagshund single_test/blub.json` — `test_job.permissions` previously appeared as a separate job. After v1 fix it was filtered out entirely, losing permission data from the detail panel.

**Scope:** Both TS (HTML DAG) and Python (text CLI). The `isSubResourceKey` predicate from v1 is reused to identify sub-resources, but instead of filtering them out, they get merged into their parent before graph building.

title: Move diff-state-styles test to tests/utils/
description: diff-state-styles.ts moved from components/ to utils/ but its test file remains at tests/components/diff-state-styles.test.ts. Move to tests/utils/diff-state-styles.test.ts to match.

title: Enforce naming conventions across codebase
description: Rename abbreviated variables (rt, ctx, cfg, pg*, s*), single-letter callbacks, vague function names (runBuild, readDistAssets, runLateralEdgeSpec, entryEdgeDiffState), boolean (wrapFlat), type (ElkLayoutResult), file renames (App.tsx, compute-search-matched-ids.test.ts), and detail-panel handleKeyDown.

title: CI: run quality gate on every push
description:
The quality gate (lint + typecheck + test) only runs on tag pushes (v*.*.*) in .gitlab-ci.yml. Regular pushes to main only get SAST/secret scanning. This means a regression can merge and sit undetected until a release is cut.

Fix: change the quality stage rules to run on every push (or at minimum every push to main), not just tags. The tag-only gate should remain for publish, but quality should run earlier.

title: expand stateful resource warnings beyond 4 types
description: Only catalogs, schemas, volumes, registered_models have data-loss warnings on delete/recreate. Other potentially stateful types — database_instances, database_catalogs, experiments, quality_monitors, secret_scopes — have no warnings. Need to research which types are actually stateful and expand _STATEFUL_RESOURCE_WARNINGS accordingly.

title: Add hook-level tests for usePhantomLeafState
description: Add tests for usePhantomLeafState hook with a small graph fixture that exercises cascade pruning: phantom leaf hidden -> parent phantom with no remaining visible children also hidden -> edges touching hidden nodes removed. Pure isPhantomLeaf is already tested; this covers the cascade algorithm. Could test via renderHook with mock Node/Edge arrays.

title: flatParentId duplication
description: flatParentId condition (workspaceEntries.length > 0 && postgresEntries.length > 0) is computed independently in both buildWorkspaceGraph and buildResourceGraph. If workspace wrapping logic changes, the copy in buildResourceGraph could drift. Fix: return flatParentId from buildWorkspaceGraph or extract a shared helper.

title: Protect GitLab release environments post first deploy
description: After the first successful tag pipeline creates the release and release-test environments in GitLab, protect them: Settings → CI/CD → Environments → edit each one → enable 'Protect this environment' → restrict deployment to Maintainers only. This prevents anyone with push access from triggering PyPI publishes without maintainer role.
close_reason: Protected environments require GitLab Premium. Existing controls sufficient: protected tags (Maintainers only), manual publish gates, OIDC environment claims verified on PyPI side.

title: epic: Staff Review R3 — Architecture, Code Quality, UX, Security
description: Third staff review round with 4 expert reviewers (architect, code quality, UX, security/perf). 29 actionable findings across HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW priorities. All findings cross-referenced against 119 closed beads to confirm novelty.

title: Stdin pipe input: accept plan JSON and open browser
description:
Accept databricks bundle plan JSON via stdin pipe and serve the webview.

USAGE PATTERN:
  databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund
  # or from a file:
  cat plan.json | dagshund

WHAT EXISTS:
- src/index.ts — Bun server entry point (currently just serves static HTML)
- parsePlanFromString in parser/ — validates JSON string into Plan

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Detect stdin pipe in src/index.ts — if process.stdin is not a TTY, read all stdin to string
2. Parse the stdin JSON using parsePlanFromString
3. Inject the parsed plan into the served HTML as a script tag: window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ = <json>
4. Auto-open the browser after server starts (use Bun.spawn or child_process to open localhost URL)
5. Frontend (src/frontend.tsx or App.tsx) reads window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ on mount and feeds it to the graph

FALLBACK: If no stdin, serve the app anyway with empty state (for dev mode / future file upload)

DATA CONTEXT:
- Plan JSON can be large (hundreds of resources with nested tasks and changes records)
- The window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ global holds the raw parsed Plan object
- The rendering layer (dagshund-qu9) reads this and passes it through the graph model

CONSTRAINTS:
- Handle parse errors gracefully — print to stderr and exit with code 1
- Use Result type from types/result.ts
- No global mutable state — read stdin once, inject into HTML response
- Server should pick an available port if default is taken (nice to have, not blocking)
close_reason: Stdin pipe input implemented: server reads stdin, parses with Zod, serves at /api/plan. Frontend fetches dynamically with loading/empty/error/ready states. Auto-opens browser on pipe.

title: fitView uses single rAF — may race with ResizeObserver
description: The initial fitView effect in flow-canvas.tsx (line ~173) uses a single requestAnimationFrame, but React Flow's ResizeObserver may not have measured node dimensions yet. The focusNodeId effect already handles this correctly with setTimeout(50ms). In HTML export mode the plan loads synchronously (no network delay), making the race more likely. Only reproduces on the Jobs tab — job group nodes require multiple ResizeObserver cycles (measure children, then resize parent container), widening the race window vs flat resource nodes. Fix: match the focusNodeId pattern — use setTimeout inside the rAF, or double-rAF.

title: CI: Playwright smoke test for browser output
description:
Add a Playwright (or similar headless browser) smoke test that:
- Builds the app (just build)
- Serves a generated HTML file with a known fixture plan
- Asserts: page renders without JS console errors, expected number of DAG nodes appear, diff state CSS classes are applied

This catches the known gap where 'just build' and 'just dev' use different Bun code paths — a passing build doesn't guarantee a working app.

Could be implemented as a subtask of dagshund-39ue (E2E HTML testing) or standalone.

title: Show compute/cluster configuration on task nodes
description:
Task nodes don't currently show which compute/cluster they run on. Databricks tasks can specify cluster configuration via job_cluster_key, existing_cluster_id, new_cluster, or compute references.

This would mean:
- Extracting compute info from the task's resource state
- Displaying it on the task node (badge, subtitle, or tooltip)
- Potentially showing shared clusters as separate nodes with edges

Low priority — not planned for any near-term release.

title: Rework README for end-user clarity
description:
The current README is developer-oriented and incomplete. Rework it into a polished, end-user-facing document that:

- Opens with a clear one-liner explaining what dagshund does and who it's for
- Shows a quick-start: install via uvx, pipe a plan, see output
- Explains both modes (text terminal output vs browser DAG visualization) with examples
- Includes screenshots or terminal output examples if possible
- Covers common workflows: reviewing bundle plan diffs, understanding job task DAGs
- Keeps it concise — no internal dev docs, no contributor guide in the main README
- Links to PyPI, any relevant Databricks docs for context
- Professional tone, approachable for data engineers who aren't CLI power users

title: Fix test_debug.py: __qualname__ includes <locals> prefix
description: test_trace_logs_entry_and_exit asserts '→ add(a=1, b=2)' but __qualname__ produces '→ test_trace_logs_entry_and_exit.<locals>.add(a=1, b=2)'. Pre-existing failure unrelated to trace additions.
close_reason: Moot — @trace decorator removed entirely in favor of sys.setprofile

title: Single-result centering triggers on diff filter alone
description: The single-result centering effect in flow-canvas.tsx fires on effectiveFilterIds, which includes diff-filter-only scenarios. When a user clicks a diff filter button (e.g. 'Added') and exactly one node matches, the viewport auto-centers on it. This was not the previous behavior — before the search bar feature, diff filter changes never triggered centering. Consider gating the centering effect so it only fires when searchQuery is non-empty, or decide this is a desirable UX improvement and keep it.
close_reason: Keeping the behavior — auto-centering on a single diff-filter match is a nice UX improvement

title: Generalize warehouse lateral edge extractor to declarative spec
description: The alert → sql_warehouse extractor uses a reverse index (API ID → resource key) because warehouse_id in alerts doesn't match the resource key pattern. Investigate whether the plan includes the warehouse ID directly — if so, this could become a simple LateralEdgeSpec like database instance edges. If not, consider adding a buildIndex option to the spec type.

title: Extract shared LateralHandles component from node components
description: lateralStyleFor helper + 4-handle JSX block is copy-pasted verbatim in resource-node.tsx and hierarchy-node.tsx. Extract into a shared LateralHandles component or a renderLateralHandles helper.

title: Test extract-resource-state.ts
description:
extract-resource-state.ts is in the critical path for browser state diffing but has zero test coverage. It's a pure function that takes plan data and produces resource state — straightforward to test with existing fixtures.

Add a test file at js/tests/graph/extract-resource-state.test.ts covering:
- Basic resource state extraction from a plan
- Edge cases (empty resources, missing fields)
- Fixture-based tests with sample-plan and complex-plan

title: Split extractPipelineTargetEdges into smaller functions
description: extractPipelineTargetEdges in extract-lateral-edges.ts is 48 lines — well over the 20-line target for pure logic functions. Split into extractPipelineCatalogEdges (direct catalog/target field) and extractPipelineIngestionEdges (ingestion_definition.objects traversal).

title: fix: preserve graph state on tab switch (hide vs unmount)
description: App.tsx lines 196-213 use conditional rendering with && to switch between DagView and ResourcesView. This fully unmounts the inactive tab, losing all state (viewport position, selected node, hover state, filter state) and re-running expensive ELK layout on switch-back. Fix: render both tabs and hide the inactive one with display:none CSS, or use a lazy-mount pattern that preserves state after first render.

title: fix: add required comments to all type assertions or replace with runtime narrowing
description: R3-HIGH-4. CLAUDE.md says 'No type assertions (as) unless absolutely unavoidable, and add a comment explaining why.' There are 7+ uncommented as-casts: (1) extractTaskState double-cast as unknown as Record (extract-tasks.ts:57) — replace with Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(task)), (2) extractDependsOnKeys as {task_key:string} (build-plan-graph.ts:84) — use 'in' narrowing, (3) buildJobIdMap as {job_id:unknown} (build-plan-graph.ts:233) — use 'in' narrowing, (4) extractDeletedTaskEntries as {old?:unknown,new?:unknown} (extract-tasks.ts:72) — type the parameter correctly as ChangeDesc, (5-7) three node.data as DagNodeData in flow-canvas.tsx:69,144,171 — add comment 'React Flow types data as Record<string,unknown>; our nodes carry DagNodeData' or create a getNodeData helper. Flagged by: Code Quality (HIGH x4).

title: Clicking nodes sometimes fails to open detail panel
description: Clicking on a node in the DAG view sometimes does not open the detail side panel. The click registers (cursor changes) but the panel doesn't appear. May be related to the existing drag-vs-click disambiguation issue (see won't-fix: 50ms symmetric debounce). Needs investigation to determine if this is the same root cause or a separate bug.

title: ELK singleton never recovers from Worker failure
description:
layout-graph.ts:24-32 — getElk() caches a single ELK instance (backed by a Web Worker) forever. If the Worker dies (OOM, crash), every subsequent layout call rejects permanently. The UI shows 'Layout failed' with no recovery path except page refresh.

Investigation (2026-03-14): Deployment plan DAGs are small (tens to low hundreds of nodes) — not enough to OOM a Worker. Page refresh is an acceptable recovery for the unlikely crash case. Closing as won't-fix.

title: display unresolved variable references from new_state.vars
description: Changes can have 'old', 'new', AND 'remote' fields. Usually remote === old, but they diverge when someone edited the resource outside the bundle (manual drift). We could detect remote != old and surface a warning in both text and browser output. Could be feature creep — the CLI already handles drift by using remote as the baseline. Evaluate whether the visual indicator adds value or just noise.

title: Phantom UC nodes for synced table source_table_full_name references
description:
When a synced_database_table references a source table via spec.source_table_full_name that doesn't exist in the bundle, create phantom nodes for the source table and its catalog/schema ancestors in the UC tree.

Example: customer_360 sits at lakebase_analytics.analytics_data.customer_360 but is a replica of dagshund.analytics.customer_profiles. If customer_profiles isn't in the bundle, it should appear as a phantom leaf under the dagshund.analytics schema (with phantom schema/catalog created as needed).

This is about UC tree structure (phantom ancestry), not the toggle-able dependency edges in dagshund-czfs.

title: epic: Staff Review R2 — Core Bugs
description: 5 bugs found during staff engineer code review round 2. Edge colors stale on theme toggle, non-job resources in DAG view, cross-job edges always unchanged, unhandled layout promise rejections, formatValue conflating undefined/null.

title: Dim non-linked nodes when lateral edges toggle is active
description: When the lateral edges toggle (Links) is active, dim nodes that have no lateral edge connections. Currently all nodes stay at full opacity when Links is toggled on — only the dashed lateral edges appear. With this change, nodes not involved in any lateral relationship should be visually de-emphasized (reduced opacity), making the lateral connections stand out. Should follow the same dimming pattern used by the diff filter (filterMatchedIds → useNodeDimming).
close_reason: Nodes without lateral edges dimmed to 0.3 when Links toggle active; isolation takes precedence

title: Add missing lateral edges for dashboards, apps, and quality_monitors
description:
Three resource types have cross-references not yet represented as lateral edges.

## dashboards → sql_warehouses
- Field: `warehouse_id` (API ID)
- Resolution: reuse the existing warehouse API ID reverse index — just add `"dashboards"` to the warehouse spec's `sourceTypes`
- Trivial change

## apps → multiple resource types
- Apps have a `resources` array with typed entries:
  - `resources[].job.id` → jobs
  - `resources[].serving_endpoint.name` → model_serving_endpoints
  - `resources[].sql_warehouse.id` → sql_warehouses (API ID reverse index)
  - `resources[].experiment.experiment_id` → experiments
  - `resources[].secret.scope` → secret_scopes
- Needs a new spec with nested traversal of the resources array
- Most complex of the three
- **Fixture**: `fixtures/apps.json` has real data for job, sql_warehouse, and secret entries
- **No real data yet** for serving_endpoint and experiment entries — implement spec but defer testing until fixtures arrive

## quality_monitors → tables
- A quality monitor monitors a specific table (`table_name` field)
- Additional table references: `baseline_table_name`, `snapshot_table_name`
- Also has `output_schema_name` for where monitor outputs are written
- Resolution depends on how table names appear in plan state (fully qualified three-part names?)
- No fixture yet

## Notes
- All follow the existing declarative LateralEdgeSpec pattern

title: Deduplicate graph building redundancies (#1-#6)
description:
Six concrete redundancies found in js/src/ graph building pipeline. All are straightforward extractions — no design decisions needed.

## 1. Extract useAsyncLayout hook (usePlanGraph / useResourceGraph)

**Files:** use-plan-graph.ts, use-resource-graph.ts
**Problem:** Both hooks are ~25 lines of identical async-state-management boilerplate. They manage loading/ready/error state, a cancellation flag, and error message extraction. The only difference is one expression — the layout function called inside the effect.

**usePlanGraph calls:** toReactFlowElements(buildPlanGraph(plan))
**useResourceGraph calls:** layoutResourceGraph(buildResourceGraph(plan))

**Fix:** Extract a useAsyncLayout(plan, transformFn) hook that accepts (plan: Plan) => Promise<LayoutResult>. Both hooks become one-line wrappers. Saves ~25 duplicated lines of state management, cancellation, and error handling.

## 2. Unify three parallel GraphEdge builders

**Files:** build-plan-graph.ts (buildDepEdge), build-resource-graph.ts (buildEdge), extract-lateral-edges.ts (buildLateralEdge)
**Problem:** Three separate functions construct GraphEdge objects with the same shape { id, source, target, label: undefined, diffState }. Each adds a minor twist:
- buildDepEdge: computes source/target from parts via buildTaskNodeId, no guard
- buildEdge: adds self-loop guard (returns undefined if source === target)
- buildLateralEdge: prefixes id with "lateral::"

**Fix:** Single buildGraphEdge(source, target, diffState?, options?) factory. Options could include { idPrefix?: string, allowSelfLoop?: boolean }. Or simpler: just buildGraphEdge(source, target, diffState?) as the base, with the self-loop guard applied at the call site (it's one line). The lateral prefix can be the idPrefix option.

## 3. Extract classifyChange helper (isTaskAdded/isTaskRemoved reimplemented 3x)

**Files:** resolve-task-diff-state.ts, build-plan-graph.ts, extract-tasks.ts
**Problem:** The logic "has new but not old → added / has old but not new → removed" is written three different ways:
- resolve-task-diff-state.ts: explicit isTaskAdded/isTaskRemoved helpers (check change.new/change.old)
- build-plan-graph.ts: inlined in resolveTaskEdgeDiffState (same checks, returns "added"/"removed"/undefined)
- extract-tasks.ts: inlined in extractDeletedTaskEntries (inverted check: skip if hasNew or no old)

**Fix:** Export a classifyChange(change: ChangeDesc): "added" | "removed" | "modified" | undefined from a shared location (resolve-task-diff-state.ts is a natural home, or a new utils/classify-change.ts). All three sites use it instead of reimplementing the same boolean logic.

## 4. Extract shared job node fields (buildJobNode / buildResourceNode overlap)

**Files:** build-plan-graph.ts (buildJobNode), build-resource-graph.ts (buildResourceNode, isJobEntry branch)
**Problem:** When building a node for a job resource, both functions compute identical fields:
- label: extractResourceName(key)
- diffState: mapActionToDiffState(entry.action)
- changes: filterJobLevelChanges(entry.changes)
- resourceState: resolveJobState(entry.new_state, entry.remote_state)
- taskChangeSummary: buildTaskChangeSummary(tasks, entry.action, entry.changes)

The only difference is nodeKind ("job" vs "resource") and how tasks are obtained (parameter vs inline resolveTaskEntries call).

**Fix:** Extract buildJobFields(key: string, entry: PlanEntry, tasks: readonly TaskEntry[]) that returns the shared field object. Each call site spreads it with its own nodeKind. Saves ~10 duplicated lines and ensures the two views stay in sync if job node logic changes.

## 5. Extract resolveAllTaskEntries helper (3-line pattern repeated twice)

**File:** build-plan-graph.ts (two locations: buildEntryGraph ~line 198 and buildRunJobEdges ~line 245)
**Problem:** The same 3-line sequence appears twice in the same file:
  const tasks = resolveTaskEntries(entry.new_state, entry.remote_state);
  const deletedTasks = extractDeletedTaskEntries(entry.changes);
  const allTasks = [...tasks, ...deletedTasks];

**Fix:** Extract resolveAllTaskEntries(entry: PlanEntry): readonly TaskEntry[] that combines live + deleted tasks in one call. Both call sites become one line.

## 6. Use existing toEdgeDiffState instead of inline reimplementation

**File:** build-plan-graph.ts lines 182-184
**Problem:** The code manually maps diffState to EdgeDiffState:
  const edgeDiffState: EdgeDiffState = resourceDiff === "added" ? "added" : resourceDiff === "removed" ? "removed" : "unchanged";

This is exactly what toEdgeDiffState(diffState) from graph-types.ts already does. The helper exists and is used in build-resource-graph.ts, but build-plan-graph.ts reimplements it inline.

**Fix:** Replace the 2-line ternary with toEdgeDiffState(resourceDiff). One-line change.

title: Harden input validation — crashes on malformed plan data
description:
## Problem

Four crash bugs in the Python package where malformed/unexpected input causes unhandled exceptions (raw tracebacks) instead of friendly DagshundError messages.

## Bug 1: `_render_resource` crashes on non-dict `changes`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 113
- The code does `for field_name, change in sorted(changes.items())` assuming `changes` is a dict
- If `changes` is a string, list, int, or any non-dict truthy value AND the action is in `UPDATE_ACTIONS`, it crashes with `AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'items'`
- The guard `if changes and action in UPDATE_ACTIONS` only checks truthiness, not type
- The `fixtures/invalid-plan.json` fixture has `"changes": "should_be_object"` — it only dodges the crash because its action is `"invalid_action_type"` (not in UPDATE_ACTIONS)
- **Fix**: Add an `isinstance(changes, dict)` check before iterating, or silently skip non-dict changes

## Bug 2: Individual change entries assumed to be dicts
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 114
- Inside the changes loop, `change.get("action", "")` assumes each change VALUE is a dict
- If a change value is a string, None, or integer → `AttributeError`
- **Fix**: Add `isinstance(change, dict)` guard or skip non-dict change values

## Bug 3: `render_text` crashes when `plan` value isn't a dict
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 201-208
- If JSON has `"plan": "not_a_dict"` or `"plan": [...]`, the truthiness check passes
- Then `_all_unchanged(plan)` calls `plan.values()` → `AttributeError`
- Same for plan entries that are None or strings — downstream functions call `.get()` on them
- **Fix**: Validate `isinstance(plan, dict)` after extracting it, raise DagshundError if not

## Bug 4: `read_plan` doesn't catch `UnicodeDecodeError`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/cli.py`, around line 54
- `open(..., encoding="utf-8")` raises `UnicodeDecodeError` on binary/corrupt files
- `UnicodeDecodeError` inherits from `ValueError`, NOT `OSError`
- Neither `except FileNotFoundError` nor `except OSError` catches it
- Users get a raw Python traceback instead of a friendly DagshundError
- **Fix**: Add `except UnicodeDecodeError` or `except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError)` that wraps it in DagshundError

## Acceptance Criteria
- All four bugs produce a friendly DagshundError message instead of a raw traceback
- `just check` passes after fixes
- Do NOT add tests in this issue — tests are tracked separately in the test hardening issue
close_reason: All four crash bugs fixed with guards + 5 new tests, 99 passing, 100% coverage

title: Generalize serving endpoint lateral edge extractor to declarative spec
description: The serving endpoint → registered_model extractor traverses config.served_entities[] to extract entity_name from each element. This is one-to-many (one endpoint → multiple models). Consider extending LateralEdgeSpec to support array-valued field extraction (e.g. extractFields returning string[]) so the runner can map over elements. The nested JSON traversal is the bespoke part — a spec with an array-aware extractField could handle this.

title: Move diff-state-styles.ts from components/ to utils/
description: diff-state-styles.ts is a pure data module (no JSX, no React) that lives in components/. It is imported by graph/layout-graph.ts and hooks/use-node-dimming.ts, creating two upward layer violations. Fix: move to utils/diff-state-styles.ts and update import paths.

title: Remove dead halfW math in chooseLateralHandles
description: In layout-graph.ts chooseLateralHandles, line 356 computes sourcePos.x + halfW - (targetPos.x + halfW) which simplifies to sourcePos.x - targetPos.x. The halfW cancels out and is dead math. Remove it for clarity.

title: Inconsistent lateral isolation naming
description: isolatedLateralNodeId vs lateralIsolatedIds use inconsistent word ordering. Pick one convention (e.g. lateralIsolated* everywhere) and rename for consistency.

title: Add aria-live to search match count
close_reason: Added aria-live=polite on persistent container for match count

title: fix: debounce node hover to prevent flickering on crowded graphs
description: On crowded graphs, moving the mouse quickly across nodes causes rapid highlight/dim toggling on every node, creating visual flicker. Add a short debounce (50-100ms) to the hover state in useNodeDimming so highlights only apply after the cursor settles on a node. Beads: dagshund-2ji

title: parseThreePartName fails on UC names containing dots
description: extract-resource-state.ts:70-78 splits on '.' and requires exactly 3 parts. Unity Catalog allows dots in backtick-quoted catalog/schema/table names. A name like my.catalog.schema.table would split into 4 parts and return undefined, making the resource an orphan in the graph. First step: investigate how databricks bundle plan -o json serializes backtick-quoted UC names to determine if this is reachable.

title: detect drift: warn when remote differs from old in changes
description: Changes can have old, new, AND remote fields. Usually remote === old, but they diverge when someone edited the resource outside the bundle (manual drift). We could detect remote != old and surface a warning in both text and browser output. Could be feature creep — the CLI already handles drift by using remote as the baseline. Evaluate whether the visual indicator adds value or just noise.

title: Snapshot tests for terminal text output
description:
Golden-file smoke tests for the installed dagshund CLI. Tests the actual packaged binary (not source) against every fixture × flag combination.

## Scope

### Golden files in tests/golden/
Store expected CLI output for every combo:
- Each fixture (sample, complex, no-changes, all-hierarchies, mixed) plain
- Filter flags: -a, -r, -m, -c with relevant fixtures
- Search filter: -f 'type:jobs', -f 'status:added', -f 'pipeline', -f '"etl_pipeline"'
- Compositions: -c -f 'type:alerts', -a -r
- HTML output: just verify file is non-empty and contains <html (no golden file for HTML content)
- --version output

CI has no TTY so output is plain text — golden files generated the same way.

### CI smoke stage
Add a smoke stage between build and release in .gitlab-ci.yml:
- Runs on every push (builds wheel locally if no build artifact)
- On tags: tests the actual build artifact from the build job
- Installs wheel in isolated uv venv, runs dagshund against fixtures
- Diffs output against golden files — any drift fails the pipeline
- Gates the release: publish-test needs smoke to pass

### just recipes
- just update-golden — regenerate all golden files from current source
- just test-golden — run golden file comparisons locally

### Cleanup
- Remove the grep-based assertions from existing subprocess smoke tests in test_cli.py that are now redundant (the golden files are strictly better)

Catches: formatting drift, missing sections, broken summaries, warning text changes, packaging issues (missing template, broken entry point).

title: fix: add aria-pressed to filter toggle buttons
description: Filter toolbar buttons visually change on active state but don't communicate to assistive tech. Fix: add aria-pressed={isActive} to each filter button in diff-filter-toolbar.tsx.

title: Chain icon on nodes with lateral edges, click to isolate that node's links
description: Add a small chain/link icon in the bottom-right corner of any node that participates in lateral edges. When clicked, the view should isolate to show only the lateral edges from/to that specific node (dimming everything else). This gives a focused view of a single node's cross-resource relationships without the noise of all lateral edges at once. Different from the global Links toggle (which shows all lateral edges) — this is per-node isolation. The icon should only appear when the global Links toggle is active, or always be visible as an affordance. UX details TBD.

title: Fix correctness bugs: focusNodeId race and missing deleted run_job edges
description:
Two behavioral bugs found in TS review:

1. **focusNodeId effect fires before layout is ready** (flow-canvas.tsx:142-154): The useEffect for focusNodeId pans to a node without checking layoutState.status === 'ready'. If the layout is still loading, getInternalNode returns undefined, onFocusComplete() clears the request, and the pan never happens. Fix: guard on layout status.

2. **buildRunJobEdges skips deleted tasks** (build-plan-graph.ts:239-266): Deleted tasks with run_job_task references don't get cross-job edges, even though their nodes appear in the graph. Fix: include deleted tasks when scanning for run_job_task references.
close_reason: Both bugs fixed and verified in browser. focusNodeId race resolved with layout status guard + setTimeout. Deleted run_job edges now included.

title: Action type vocabulary duplicated across three locations
description: Action types are defined in three places: Python types.py action_to_diff_state() match statement, Python text.py _ACTIONS dict, and JS plan-schema.ts knownActionTypes. All handle unknowns gracefully (wildcard/catch), but a new Databricks action type requires updating all three. Fix: add a 'three places to update' comment in each location pointing to the others, or consider a shared constant file that both sides reference during review.

title: refactor: extract shared loadFixture helper from test files
description: R3-LOW-28. loadFixture function is duplicated in js/tests/graph/build-plan-graph.test.ts and build-resource-graph.test.ts with identical implementation: reads fixture file, parses with parsePlanJson, throws on failure. Fix: create js/tests/helpers/load-fixture.ts with the shared function and import from both test files. Flagged by: Code Quality (HIGH).

title: Lateral edges between synced tables and their source-table phantoms
description: Add lateral edges connecting synced_database_tables resources to their source-table phantom nodes. Currently synced tables and their source-table phantoms both appear in the UC tree but have no visual connection — they're linked only implicitly by sharing a schema parent. A lateral edge from the synced table resource node to its source-table:: phantom (derived from spec.source_table_full_name) would make the data lineage relationship explicit. These would be extracted in extractLateralEdges or a similar step, using the same source_table_full_name field that creates the phantom.

title: Lateral edge extraction: consistency and collision fixes
description:
Three related issues from review:

1. Missing nodeIds.has(sourceNodeId) guard in extractDatabaseInstanceEdges and extractServingEndpointModelEdges — inconsistent with extractWarehouseEdges which does the check. Low risk since nodeIdByResourceKey is built from allNodes, but should be consistent.

2. Lateral edge IDs use the same source→target format as hierarchy edges. If a lateral edge coincides with a depends_on or hierarchy edge, duplicate IDs would confuse React Flow. Prefix lateral IDs with 'lateral::' to guarantee uniqueness.

3. extractPipelineTargetEdges could emit duplicate edges for the same pipeline→schema pair if ingestion_definition.objects has redundant entries. Add a seen-set dedup within the function.

title: LateralDependencyEntry and PhantomContextEntry share fields
description: Both types share label, resourceKey, and resourceType fields. LateralDependencyEntry adds nodeId and diffState. Could extract a shared base type, but they serve different conceptual purposes so keeping separate is also defensible.

title: Clean up useNodeDimming exports and unused return fields
description: NodeDimmingResult type is exported but never imported outside use-node-dimming.ts. Several return fields (isDimmed, dimOpacity, isHovered, isSelected, isFilterHighlighted) are computed but never destructured by any caller. Unexport the type and consider removing or lazy-computing unused fields.

title: Add paneClickDistance={5} for consistency with nodeClickDistance
description: nodeClickDistance={5} was added to fix node click suppression from d3-drag. The same d3-zoom click suppression can affect pane clicks (onPaneClick clears selection). Add paneClickDistance={5} to ReactFlow for consistency. Lower priority since pane clicks are less frequent and less affected.

title: isUnknownRecord type guard does not exclude arrays
description: unknown-record.ts:11-12 — isUnknownRecord checks typeof value === 'object' && value \!== null but does not exclude arrays. An array would pass the guard and be cast to Record<string, unknown>. Not reachable from current data sources (Databricks plan state objects are always plain objects at the checked nesting levels), but defensively incorrect. Fix: add && \!Array.isArray(value) to the guard.

title: task: theming infrastructure — CSS variables, dark/light toggle, OS preference
description:
Foundation for the UI overhaul. Set up the theming system that everything else builds on. Part of epic dagshund-4y6.

## Deliverables
- CSS custom properties (design tokens) for colors, shadows, borders, radii — defined for both dark and light modes
- Databricks-inspired color palette: muted blues/grays, professional feel, dagshund identity
- Tailwind config extended with theme tokens where appropriate
- Dark/light mode toggle component (sun/moon icon button)
- OS preference detection via prefers-color-scheme media query
- Manual override persisted to localStorage
- Diff state colors (green/red/amber) tuned for readability in both modes
- Base body/background/text colors applied globally

title: refactor: rename FORBIDDEN_RESPONSE factory to createForbiddenResponse
description: R3-LOW-25. FORBIDDEN_RESPONSE in index.ts:58 uses SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (suggesting a constant) but is actually a factory function () => new Response('Forbidden', {status:403}). Per naming conventions, functions should be camelCase verb-first. Fix: rename to createForbiddenResponse. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: Add test cases for chooseLateralHandles edge cases
description: chooseLateralHandles only has one indirect test case via toLateralFlowEdges (source above target). Add cases for: source below target, same Y position, and horizontal-dominant separation where all handle combos have equal dy.

title: CLI warnings for dangerous plan actions
description:
Emit warnings for plan actions that have destructive real-world consequences users might not expect.

Priority case: schema recreate. A 'recreate' action on a Unity Catalog schema drops all tables in that schema — this is catastrophic and not obvious from the plan output alone. dagshund should surface a loud warning when it sees this.

Warning categories (start small, expand later):
- Schema recreate → warns about table loss
- Mass deletes (>N resources with action=delete) → warns about blast radius
- Any 'recreate' action → warns that resource will be destroyed and re-created

Output:
- Warnings print above the resource summary in text mode
- Distinct from the diff output — visually separated, maybe with a ⚠ prefix or similar
- Should work with --detailed-exitcode (warnings don't change exit code — or maybe they should? design decision)
- Could later integrate into browser mode as a warnings panel

Start with schema recreate detection. That's the one that bites people.

title: Agent Skill: SKILL.md + --install-skill CLI flag + marketplace publish
description:
Package dagshund as a Claude Code Agent Skill so AI agents can discover, install, and use it automatically.

## Deliverables

1. **SKILL.md** — Agent skill definition with:
   - YAML frontmatter (name, description, trigger patterns for deploy/bundle/DAG contexts)
   - Installation recipe (uvx dagshund)
   - Usage recipes (text mode, browser mode, piping from databricks bundle plan)
   - Output interpretation guidance

2. **--install-skill CLI flag** — New CLI option that:
   - Copies bundled SKILL.md to .claude/skills/dagshund/SKILL.md in current project
   - Optionally supports --global to install to ~/.claude/skills/
   - SKILL.md bundled as package data in the wheel

3. **Marketplace publish** — Submit to claude-plugins.dev registry
   - Skill auto-triggers when users discuss Databricks deploys, bundle plans, DAGs
   - Includes uvx install step so agent handles everything end-to-end

## Context

Suggested by Databricks Asset Bundles team. Two user journeys:
- Manual: uvx dagshund --install-skill → agent knows dagshund
- Marketplace: user asks about deploys → agent discovers skill → installs dagshund → runs it

## Notes

- Source is on GitLab, not GitHub — registry indexes from URLs, not GitHub-specific
- SKILL.md is the first step — both CLI flag and marketplace need it

title: Structured search query DSL
description:
Add a lightweight query DSL for combining search filters. Currently search supports three modes (fuzzy, "exact", type:badge) but they're mutually exclusive — you can't combine them.

Potential syntax:
- type:pipeline name:ingest — AND combination of badge + name match
- type:volume status:added — badge + diff state
- name:"exact match" type:job — exact name + badge

Design questions:
- How far to take this? Full boolean (&&, ||, NOT) or just implicit AND of field:value pairs?
- Should status/diff filters (added/modified/removed) be queryable this way too, merging the toolbar filters into the DSL?
- Parser complexity vs. value — the current three-mode dispatch in computeSearchMatchedIds is simple. A DSL parser is more code to maintain.
- Discoverability — how does the user learn the syntax? Placeholder text, tooltip, help popover?

Probably P4 until the simple search feels limiting in practice.

title: display change reason field in detail panel
description: ChangeDesc has a reason field (backend_default, server_side_default, alias, remote_already_set, empty, empty_struct, custom, !drop) that is parsed but never displayed. Could show as a tooltip or annotation on skip changes to explain why they're skipped. The !drop reason has special semantics (field being dropped) and might warrant filtering. Low priority — informational only.

title: Extract viewport positioning effects into useViewportPositioning hook
description: flow-canvas.tsx has three viewport-positioning effects (fit-view on lines 179-199, focus-node on lines 206-217, single-match centering on lines 279-295) plus rfInstanceRef/hasFittedRef/hasFitted that form a cohesive concern. Extracting into a useViewportPositioning hook would remove ~60 lines and 3 effects from FlowCanvas while keeping a clear interface: { onInit, handleFitView, hasFitted }.

title: CLI --changes-only flag to suppress unchanged resources
description:
Add diff state filter flags that mirror the browser UI's Added/Modified/Removed toggle buttons.

Flags:
- -a / --added — show only created resources
- -m / --modified — show only updated/recreated/resized/update_id resources
- -r / --removed — show only deleted resources
- -c / --changes-only — shorthand for -a -m -r (all changes, no unchanged)

Flags compose with each other: -a -m shows added + modified.
When --filter DSL ships (dagshund-thdy), these compose as an additional layer.

Behavior:
- No flags = current behavior (show everything)
- With flags: hide resources whose diff state is not in the visible set
- Hide entire type groups where no resources pass the filter
- Group headers show (changed/total) when filtering reduces the count
- Summary line always counts ALL resources (unchanged count is not lost)
- No effect on browser mode (-o) or --detailed-exitcode logic

Implementation:
- Model as a set of visible diff states passed to render_text
- _ActionConfig.changed maps to diff states: create→added, delete→removed, update/recreate/resize/update_id→modified, skip/empty→unchanged
- _print_resource_groups filters entries against the visible set
- CLI parses flags into the set; empty set means show all

title: buildJobIdMap does not index string-typed numeric job IDs
description:
build-plan-graph.ts:209-246 — buildJobIdMap indexes by numeric job_id only. The Zod schema for run_job_task accepts z.union([z.string(), z.number()]) for job_id, so a string numeric ID is theoretically valid.

Investigation (2026-03-14): Live plan output from Databricks CLI v0.288.0 confirms job_id is always int in remote_state (e.g. 949076684616381). The string branch in the union exists for bundle interpolation strings like ${resources.jobs.data_quality_pipeline.id} in new_state, which are handled separately by the typeof === 'string' check in buildRunJobEdges. A string-typed numeric ID is not produced by the API. Closing as unreachable.

title: feat: Escape key to close detail panel
description: Detail panel can only be closed by clicking the X button. No Escape key handler. Fix: add useEffect keydown listener for Escape that calls handleClosePanel. Universal UI convention.

title: CI: end-to-end HTML output testing
description:
Add a formal end-to-end testing suite in CI that exercises the full HTML output pipeline:

- Run dagshund with representative fixture plans and -o to produce HTML files
- Validate the generated HTML (well-formed, expected structure, embedded JS/CSS present)
- Optionally open in a headless browser (Playwright or similar) to verify:
  - The page renders without JS errors
  - DAG nodes and edges appear for known fixture inputs
  - Diff highlighting classes are applied correctly
  - Interactive features (click, hover, zoom) respond
- Snapshot or assertion-based checks so regressions are caught automatically
- Integrate into the existing 'just check' / CI quality gate

This covers the gap between unit tests (Python logic, React components) and manual browser verification.

title: Toggle to show/hide phantom leaf nodes
description:
Add a toggle button to show/hide phantom leaf nodes (source-table:: phantoms, future database-instance:: phantoms). Default: visible.

When toggled off, hide:
- The phantom leaf node itself
- The hierarchy edge from its parent (e.g. schema → source-table)
- Any lateral edge targeting it (e.g. synced_table → source-table)

Must NOT trigger ELK re-layout — just visibility toggling like the lateral edges toggle.

Hierarchy phantoms (catalog::, schema::, postgres-project::, postgres-branch::) are structural and always visible — not affected by this toggle.

Implementation notes:
- Tag phantom nodes with tier info during creation, or filter by ID prefix (source-table::, future database-instance::, etc.)
- Follow the same pattern as the lateral edges toggle for no-relayout show/hide

title: Audit and improve handling of skip/unchanged resources
description:
The text renderer shows 'skip' to the user (e.g. '8 skip') which is Databricks internal terminology. Users see 'unchanged' — we should display that instead.

## Scope
1. **Text mode**: 'skip' action renders as '(skip)' and '8 skip' in summary. Should say 'unchanged'.
2. **Browser mode**: verify how skip resources render in the DAG visualization — are they styled as 'unchanged'?
3. **All-skip plan**: when every resource is skip (no changes), the CLI currently renders the full plan with all resources listed. Should it show a 'no changes' summary instead? Or at least suppress the resource list?

## Fixture
`fixtures/no-changes-plan.json` — real Databricks output where nothing changed, all 8 resources have action 'skip'.

## Current behavior
```
dagshund plan (v2, cli 0.288.0)

  alerts (1)
    alerts/stale_pipeline_alert  (skip)
  ...
   8 skip
```

## Related
- dagshund-qwui: detailed exit codes (needs to distinguish all-skip from real changes)
- dagshund-cfx: empty plan error (different from all-skip — empty plan = no plan key)
close_reason: Python text mode: skip→unchanged mapping, all-unchanged short-circuit, clean style map. Browser side tracked in dagshund-wtjp.

title: fix: resolve edge type bezier vs smoothstep conflict
description: layout-graph.ts:301 hardcodes edges as type:'bezier'. App.tsx:34 and resources-view.tsx:28 set defaultEdgeOptions type:'smoothstep'. Since individual edge type overrides the default, smoothstep never takes effect — either the edges should be smoothstep (remove type from toFlowEdges) or the default is dead config (remove from DEFAULT_EDGE_OPTIONS). Determine intended behavior and make consistent.

3. **js/src/utils/unknown-record.ts** — `isUnknownRecord`, `getUnknownProp` type guards used across parser/graph layers. Test with null, undefined, arrays, objects, primitives.

## Weak assertions to fix

- `build-plan-graph.test.ts:205` — `toBeGreaterThan(0)` on deterministic fixture → exact count
- `build-plan-graph.test.ts:221-222` — regression test uses `toBeGreaterThan(0)` → exact counts

description:
resource-key.ts:2-5 returns segments[segments.length - 1], so resources.jobs.data.pipeline → 'pipeline'. Python's parse_resource_key correctly joins remaining segments: 'data.pipeline'. This affects node labels in the resource graph and causes a search DSL parity gap.

Investigation (2026-03-14): Dotted YAML resource keys (e.g. etl_pipeline.hey.hot.pop) pass databricks bundle validate but crash the CLI with a nil pointer dereference in ResourceJob.PrepareState (job.go:51) on both plan and deploy. The CLI never produces plan JSON with dotted keys — dagshund would never see them. Additionally, UC object names cannot contain dots per Unity Catalog naming rules. Closing as unreachable.

Reference: Databricks CLI v0.288.0 panic in dresources.(*ResourceJob).PrepareState — could be filed as a bug at https://github.com/databricks/cli but is unlikely to be 'fixed' by allowing dots.

title: Fix action:null inconsistency across three functions
description:
## Problem

Three functions in `src/dagshund/text.py` disagree on how to interpret `"action": null` (JSON null / Python None) in a resource entry, producing inconsistent output.

## The Three Functions and Their Behavior

### 1. `_render_resource` (around line 101)
- Uses `entry.get("action", "")` → returns `None` (not the default, because key exists with value None)
- `_display_action(None)` → returns `None` unchanged (not in `_DISPLAY_ACTIONS`, falls through)
- Resource displayed as `? jobs/test  (None)` with unknown-action style

### 2. `_count_by_action` (around line 138)
- Uses `entry.get("action") or "unchanged"` → `None or "unchanged"` → `"unchanged"`
- Summary counts it as "unchanged"

### 3. `_all_unchanged` (around line 194)
- Uses `entry.get("action", "")` → returns `None`
- Checks `None in ("skip", "")` → `False`
- So it does NOT consider the resource unchanged, meaning the full resource listing is shown

## Result
- Summary line says "unchanged"
- Resource listing shows "?/None" with unknown-action styling
- `_all_unchanged` says "not all unchanged" so the listing IS shown (contradicting the summary)

## Reproduction
```python
from dagshund.text import render_text
import json
render_text(json.dumps({"plan": {"resources.jobs.test": {"action": None}}}))
```

## Design Decision Needed
What should `action: null` mean? Options:
1. Treat it the same as `action: ""` and `action: "skip"` → "unchanged"
2. Treat it as an unknown/error action

Either way, all three functions must agree. Recommendation: treat null the same as missing/empty → "unchanged", since Databricks CLI may omit the field or set it to null for no-op resources.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three functions (`_render_resource`, `_count_by_action`, `_all_unchanged`) handle `action: null` consistently
- The chosen behavior is explicit, not accidental (e.g., normalize early rather than relying on falsy checks)
- `just check` passes
close_reason: Aligned _count_by_action to use same extraction pattern as _render_resource and _all_unchanged — null actions now consistently treated as unknown

title: Add missing task type badges: clean_rooms_notebook, dashboard, power_bi
description:
Three Databricks task types from https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/bundles/job-task-types are missing explicit entries in TASK_TYPE_BADGES (js/src/utils/task-type.ts):

- clean_rooms_notebook_task → suggest badge: "clean room"
- dashboard_task → suggest badge: "dashboard"
- power_bi_task → suggest badge: "power bi"

The fallback in extractTaskTypeBadge() handles them (strips _task, converts underscores to spaces), so they render fine — but adding explicit badges keeps the map authoritative and gives us shorter labels (e.g. "clean room" instead of "clean rooms notebook").

Also add test cases in js/tests/utils/task-type.test.ts.

title: Add unit tests for 3 untested pure functions
description:
Three pure exported functions lack isolated unit tests. All are indirectly exercised through integration tests but have no direct proof of correctness.

## Functions

   - Test cases: empty edges, depends-on only, depended-on-by only, both directions, non-lateral edge filtering, missing node references

2. **toEdgeDiffState** — `js/src/types/graph-types.ts:57` (2 lines)
   - Maps DiffState to EdgeDiffState (drops 'modified' → 'unchanged')

3. **buildGraphEdge** — `js/src/types/graph-types.ts:69` (5 lines)
   - Pure edge constructor with ID format and optional diffState default
   - Used pervasively but no test proves ID format or defaults

## Context
Found during test crusade audit. Coverage numbers: Python 92%, TypeScript 93% lines. These are the only pure-logic gaps — everything else is either side-effectful (ELK Worker, subprocess, browser open) or presentation layer (hooks/components).

End-leaf nodes show a dot on the right. Only render the target Handle when the node has at least one incoming edge, and the source Handle when it has at least one outgoing edge.

title: fix: clean up modified task detail panel — remove redundant diff state display
description:
The detail panel for modified tasks is visually noisy with redundant information:

## Current behavior
- Top bar shows task name + "modified" action badge
- Below that, a "Modified" text banner repeats the same info
- Then the actual changed fields
- Then an "Unchanged" separator at the bottom

## Desired behavior
- Top bar: task name + "modified" action badge (keep as-is)
- Body: "Modified" and "Unchanged" section dividers organizing the fields
- No extra banner/block that redundantly restates the diff state

Added and removed nodes are clean — single wrapped block with a tag. Modified tasks should follow the same clean pattern but with section dividers to separate changed vs unchanged fields.

title: Smarter diff display for modified resources in detail panel
description:
When clicking a modified node, the detail panel currently shows full before/after arrays for fields like depends_on. For example:

- [{ task_key: check_nulls }]
+ [{ task_key: check_nulls }, { task_key: check_duplicates }]

This should be reduced to just showing the meaningful delta:
+ check_duplicates

However, this is not always straightforward — need to think about validity/edge cases:
- What if items were reordered but not added/removed?
- What if items were both added AND removed in the same array?
- What about arrays of primitives vs arrays of objects?
- How do we match identity across old/new arrays (by which key)?
- Nested object diffs within array items

Needs a design pass before implementation to handle these cases correctly.

title: epic: UI overhaul — Databricks-inspired design with dark/light mode
description:
Graphical overhaul of the entire application to bring a cohesive, polished visual style inspired by the Databricks UI aesthetic.

## Goals
- Adopt a Databricks-inspired design language (clean lines, subtle shadows, professional color palette)
- Implement both dark mode and light mode with a user toggle
- Consistent styling across all views (DAG, resources, detail panel, upload screen)

## Scope
- Theming infrastructure (CSS variables or Tailwind theme, mode toggle, persistence)
- Restyle all components: nodes, edges, panels, buttons, inputs, headers
- Diff state colors adapted for both modes (green/red/amber must be readable in both)
- React Flow canvas styling (background, controls, minimap)
- Typography, spacing, and layout polish

title: Show sync relationship context on phantom sync-target nodes in detail panel

Phantom sync-target nodes (e.g. sync-target::dagshund.analytics.customer_360) show an empty or unhelpful detail panel when clicked. These nodes exist because a synced_database_table references them as its UC destination.

The detail panel should show:

title: Type safety: add comments to all as casts, replace any with typed import
description:

5. **Uncommented as casts in test files** (build-resource-graph.test.ts, layout-graph.test.ts): Discriminated union narrowing casts in tests. Add brief comments.

title: fix: simplify job detail panel in resources tab
description: When clicking a job node on the Resources tab, the detail panel is overwhelming. It shows the full Modified section with all task-level changes (depends_on diffs, new tasks, field changes) AND the full Unchanged section with every field (deployment, environments, all tasks with their full config).
